content.full_text:
FPPC

JAN 0 8 10 AM '90

OFFICE OF THE

CITY ATTORNEY

January 4, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

This is a request for advice concerning potential conflicts of interest of
a city councilmember in relation to the City's unreinforced masonry building
program.

Facts

State law relating to seismic safety required cities to identify unreinforced
masonry buildings prior to January 1, 1990, and to report this information
to the state. South San Francisco has done this. State law further requires
the city to adopt a mitigation program; however, the type of program is left
to the discretion of the city and may be as simple as notifying each building
owner that the building is potentially hazardous or as complex as requiring
an engineering analysis and retrofitting the building to conform to current
seismic requirements. South San Francisco is beginning to formulate a
mitigation program.

The City has identified some 25 privately owned buildings in roughly a three-
block area downtown which could be subject to a mitigation program under the
law. It also has been suggested that such a mitigation program should not
be limited to only the buildings of concern under the state law.

Once the staff finishes formulating mitigation alternatives it intends to
present the options to the Council at a study session. Depending upon what
occurs, it is likely that an ordinance will then be drafted and presented
to the Council for formal action.

Recently elected councilmember John Penna is a real estate broker. His
business, Penna Realty, deals with many properties throughout the City and
serves as the property manager for one of the buildings on the list. His

City Hall Annex          315 Maple Avenue 94080          (415) 877-8515
                    P.O. Box 711 94083

Kathryn E. Donovan
January 4, 1990
Page 2

business also has been assisting in seeking a tenant for another one
of the buildings on the list, although there is no on-going business
relationship. He, along with the other councilmembers, is interested
in devising a program which will improve public safety, particularly
in light of the recent earthquake.

Questions

1. May Councilmember Penna participate in devising and adopting
a program to deal with the identified unreinforced masonry
buildings?

2. May Councilmember Penna participate in determining if a
seismic safety program should be applied City-wide and
participate in devising and adopting such a program?

Thank you in advance for the time and attention devoted to this request.
If you need any additional information or clarification, do not hesitate
to contact me.


Very truly yours,

[signature]
VALERIE J. ARMENTO
City Attorney


VJA/jh

FPPC

APR 24 10 02 AM '90

OFFICE OF THE

CITY ATTORNEY

April 23, 1990

Jeevan Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804

Re: Letter No. 90-032

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

On January 4, 1990 I sent a request for advice, which the FPPC received on
July 8. By way of a January 10 letter from Kathryn Donovan I was informed
that you were assigned to this matter and that typically a response is
received within 21 working days.

On February 1, you and I had a telephone conversation during which you sought
clarification of some points. I have not heard from you since, despite a
phone call approximately one month ago and another last week.

My City Council will be holding a public hearing on the matter of adopting an
unreinforced masonry structure ordinance on May 23, 1990. Whether or not one
of my Councilmembers may participate in that is the primary reason the opinion
was requested. It would be extremely helpful if we could have a response
before that date.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
VALERIE J. ARMENTO
City Attorney

VJA/mm

315 MAPLE AVENUE - P.O. BOX 711 - 94083
TELEPHONE: (415) 877-8515
